_schema._doc: Geometric Progression (GP) buildup-factor coefficients tabulated in Harima, Sakamoto, Tanaka & Kawai, 'Validity of the Geometric Progression Formula in Approximating Gamma-Ray Buildup Factors', Nuclear Science and Engineering 94 (1986). One entry per (material, energy_MeV) pair.

_format.name: human-readable material label (matches `materials[name]` below)
_format.energies_MeV: list of photon energies in MeV at which coefficients are tabulated
_format.coefficients: object keyed by `<energy_MeV>` (string) -> {a, b, c, d, xi} where K(mut) = c*mut^a + d*[tanh(mut/xi - 2) - tanh(-2)] / [1 - tanh(-2)] and B(mut) = 1 + (b-1)*(K^mut - 1)/(K - 1) for K != 1, else 1 + (b-1)*mut
_format.valid_range_mfp: optional [low, high] mfp range over which the GP fit is considered valid (Harima 1986 fits go to 40 mfp by default)

_schema._units: Energies in MeV. Coefficients are dimensionless. mut is dimensionless mean-free-paths (mfp).
_schema._quantity: Dose buildup factor for a point isotropic source in an infinite homogeneous medium. Use AP geometry dose-AP-photon to compare against this.
_schema._population_status: EMPTY -- coefficients must be entered from Harima 1986 NSE 94 (Tables I-VI). The script will report 'no entries to compare' until at least one (material, energy) is filled in.
